title: 11. Observability & monitoring — logs, metrics, traces
description: JSON logs, Prometheus /metrics, OTEL opt-in, Grafana/Prometheus compose.
story:
Observability answers: what is broken, where, and why — from signals the system emits.

Three pillars (industry):
• LOGS — structured JSON events with request_id (observability/logging_setup.py)
• METRICS — counters/histograms on /metrics when enable_prometheus_metrics=true
• TRACES — OpenTelemetry spans when otel_enabled=true (default false in demo)

Configs: monitoring/prometheus/, grafana provisioning, docker-compose.observability.yaml, k8s/monitoring/.
Claim 'observability is done' only when scrape + dashboard + alerts are real.
one_liner: Logs + Prometheus metrics + optional OTEL; prove with scrape targets and alerts.
why_it_matters: Diagnose/promote latency and error rates need numbers.
PII redaction in logs is a security requirement.
say_aloud: We emit JSON logs with request correlation ids.
Prometheus scrapes /metrics when metrics are enabled.
A metrics endpoint without scrape config is only a stub.

beats.title: Metrics endpoint pattern
beats.say_after: Enable metrics, scrape them, alert on SLOs.

concepts.say_aloud: Logs for events, metrics for aggregates, traces for request paths.

further_reading.title: Google SRE Book — Monitoring Distributed Systems

final_boss: Draw log → metrics → trace path for one /diagnose request.
